Caption: The problem under investigation: a 1 D hot rigid perfectly conductor rough punch is squeezed against an elastic half-plane.

The deformed profile of an elastic substrate when a hot rigid rough profile is squeezed against it. Results are given for different temperatures of the rigid indenter and for self-affine fractal profiles with N = 100 ( m 2 = 4 . 2 × 1 0 − 4 ), H = 0 . 8 , and h rms = 1 μ m. A projection of the contact regions is also shown at the bottom of the plot to help visualization of the growing contact areas.

The actual contact area A / A 0 as a function of the dimensionless approach Δ / h rms for different values of the temperature of the rigid indenter. All results are given for self-affine fractal profiles with N = 100 ( m 2 = 4 . 2 × 1 0 − 4 ), H = 0 . 8 , and h rms = 1 μ m.

The actual contact area A / A 0 (a) and the interfacial mean separation u ̄ / h rms (b) as functions of the average contact pressure σ 0 / E ∗ for different values of the temperature of the rigid indenter. All results are given for self-affine fractal profiles with N = 100 ( m 2 = 4 . 2 × 1 0 − 4 ), H = 0 . 8 , and h rms = 1 μ m.

The actual contact area A / A 0 (a) and the interfacial mean separation u ̄ / h rms (b) as functions of the average contact pressure σ 0 / E ∗ for different values of the root mean square slope m 2 of the rigid profile at α T 0 = 1 . 4 × 1 0 − 4 . All results are given for self-affine fractal profiles with H = 0 . 8 and h rms = 1 μ m.

The heat flow Q α / K h rms as a function of the average contact pressure σ 0 / E ∗ for different values of the temperature of the rigid indenter (a), and root mean square slope m 2 of the rough profile (b). All results are given for self-affine fractal profiles with H = 0 . 8 and h rms = 1 μ m.

The superficial temperature distribution for different values of the applied penetration Δ / h rms . The rigid punch is assumed to be at a temperature of 2 ° C and results are given for a self-affine fractal profile with N = 100 ( m 2 ≈ 4 . 2 × 1 0 − 4 ), H = 0 . 8 , and h rms = 1 μ m. In addition, the substrate is assumed to be initially at a uniform temperature T sub = 0 ° C .

The thermal contact resistance TCR K h r m s as a function of the contact pressure σ 0 / E ∗ for different values of the temperature α T 0 of the rigid punch (a), and root mean square slope m 2 of the rough profile (b). In both plots data are given by the logarithm with base 10 . Markers refer to numerical data, while dashed lines in Fig. 8b refer to the fit of data with the law A σ 0 / E ∗ − r . All results are given for self-affine fractal profiles with H = 0 . 8 and h rms = 1 μ m.
